steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
$$x^{2} - \frac{25}{81} = 0 \implies x^{2} = \frac{25}{81}$$
Take square roots: $x = \pm \frac{5}{9}$. Because the right side is positive, both the positive and negative roots are valid real solutions.

steps.title: Count the solutions
steps.content: There are two distinct real solutions: $x = \frac{5}{9}$ and $x = -\frac{5}{9}$. That matches Choice C — Exactly two.
